lessonTitle: Be Strong and of a Good Courage
quickInsight: God didn't wait for Joshua to feel ready. He just said GO.

quotableVerses.text: Have not I commanded thee? Be strong and of a good courage; be not afraid, neither be thou dismayed: for the Lord thy God is with thee whithersoever thou goest.
Sanctify yourselves: for to morrow the Lord will do wonders among you.
Choose you this day whom ye will serve; … but as for me and my house, we will serve the Lord.
And it shall come to pass, as soon as the soles of the feet of the priests that bear the ark of the Lord … shall rest in the waters of Jordan, that the waters of Jordan shall be cut off.

sections.title: The Story
items: Moses is dead. Joshua — his assistant, not a prophet yet — gets tapped to lead an entire nation into enemy territory. No training montage. Just 'go.'
First obstacle: the Jordan River is flooding. God says march the priests straight into it. The water doesn't part UNTIL their feet hit the water.
Next: Jericho, a walled fortress. God's battle plan? Walk in circles and blow trumpets. It sounds ridiculous — until the walls collapse.
Then disaster: a guy named Achan secretly stashes stolen loot, and the whole army loses their next battle because of ONE person's hidden sin.
At the end of his life, Joshua draws a line: 'Choose you this day whom ye will serve.' No more riding the fence.

sections.title: Why This Matters to You
items: Joshua was probably terrified. God told him 'be strong and of good courage' THREE TIMES in nine verses — you don't repeat that to someone who already feels brave. If you feel unready for the priesthood, for seminary, for standing up — you're in good company.

The Jordan River moment is real life: God often asks you to step into the water BEFORE He parts it. You bear your testimony before you feel it burning. You invite someone to church before you know they'll say yes. Faith means moving your feet first.

Achan's story hits different when you realize your hidden stuff — the stuff on your phone, the lies you think are harmless — doesn't just affect you. It affects your quorum, your family, your team. Sin is never just private.
Joshua 24:15 isn't a gentle suggestion. It's a 'pick a side' moment. Every day at school, online, with your boys — you're choosing who you serve whether you realize it or not.

sections.title: Talk Starters
items: When's the last time you had to do something hard before you felt ready — and it worked out? What made you step up?
God's plan for Jericho made zero military sense. Has the Holy Ghost ever prompted you to do something that seemed weird or pointless but turned out to matter?
Be honest: is there an 'Achan moment' in your life right now — something hidden you know is dragging you down? What would it take to let it go?
If you had to pick ONE thing you're choosing to serve right now based on where your time actually goes — what would it be? Is that the answer you want?

sections.title: How to Teach It
items: Feet-in-the-water demo: Fill a shallow pan with water. Have a young man step toward it — explain nothing happens until you MOVE. Then read Joshua 3:13, 15. Ask: 'Where in your life is God waiting for you to step in before He acts?'

The Jericho method: Give them an impossible task (stack cups into a tower blindfolded, following only your voice instructions). Debrief: 'God's instructions don't always make sense. Joshua's army looked insane marching in circles. But obedience unlocked the miracle.'

Hidden weight: Put a small rock in someone's shoe at the start of class. After a few minutes ask how it feels. That's Achan's stolen goods — small, hidden, and it wrecks everything. Read Joshua 7:10–13.

sections.title: Do This at Mutual
items: Courage challenge course: Set up a simple obstacle course where each station requires trusting a partner's voice while blindfolded. At each station, read one of the 'be strong and of good courage' verses. End with a group discussion: 'Where do you need courage right now — and who's got your back?'

Choose-you-this-day wall: Give each young man a card. One side: write what the WORLD is asking you to serve right now. Other side: write what GOD is asking. Tape them up. No one has to share — but let them see they're not the only ones fighting the same battle.

sections.title: Challenge for the Week
items: This week, do ONE thing that scares you before you feel ready — bear your testimony, talk to someone at school you've been avoiding, delete something off your phone, tell your parents something you've been holding back. Step into the river. Then watch what God does.
Memorize Joshua 1:9. Just that one verse. Say it to yourself before school every morning this week. See if it changes how you walk into the building.

sections.title: Restoration Connection
items: Joshua's name (Yehoshua) literally means 'Jehovah saves' — it's the same name as Jesus. Joshua led Israel through the Jordan into a new life. Jesus leads us through the waters of baptism into covenant life. Same pattern, same God.
President Nelson's 'Hear Him' talk (April 2020) echoes Joshua 1:8 — immerse yourself in the word of God and everything changes. Scripture study isn't homework; it's how God talks to you personally.
1 Nephi 15:23–24 — Nephi taught that holding to the word of God keeps you from falling. Joshua got the same counsel 2,600 years earlier. The pattern hasn't changed because it WORKS.
